items.title: Indonesia: Index review focus and fiscal strain – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Radhika Rao highlights sharp swings in Indonesian assets, with the Rupiah, bonds and equities rebounding after recent losses.

items.title: Forex Today: US yields drop as labor cracks offset inflation
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) dropped to the 98.80 price region after the United States (US) Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report confirmed that inflation remains sticky, reinforcing the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) cautious stance.

items.title: Taiwan: Goldilocks outlook faces energy shock – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Ma Tieying assesses Taiwan’s 2Q 2026 macro outlook, noting that the economy entered 2026 with strong growth and low inflation, supported by AI-related exports and easing US tariff pressures.

items.title: WTI trims gains as improving sentiment around US-Iran truce weighs
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil reverses course on Thursday, erasing earlier gains as a fresh wave of optimism sweeps through global markets, easing concerns over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Asia FX: Geopolitics and cautious positioning – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Michael Wan highlights that Asian currencies have held up alongside a weaker Dollar despite renewed tensions around the fragile two-week ceasefire in the Middle East.

items.title: Global manufacturing: PMI drop highlights bottlenecks – ABN AMRO
items.summary: ABN AMRO's Senior Economist Arjen van Dijkhuizen highlights that global manufacturing PMI slipped from 51.8 in February to 51.3 in March, largely linked to the Iran conflict.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average extends ceasefire rally as Netanyahu opens Lebanon talks
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) climbed around 300 points on Thursday, or roughly 0.7%, extending a two-day rally that has seen the index recover sharply from its war-era lows.

items.title: Canadian Dollar rebounds, but is the ceasefire rally already fading?
items.summary: USD/CAD has shed nearly a full percent since the start of the week, sliding from the 1.3965 area to trade near the 1.3800 handle on Thursday.

items.title: Israel signals direct talks with Lebanon on Hezbollah disarmament
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced Thursday that he has ordered the start of direct negotiations with Lebanon "as soon as possible," with talks expected to center on disarming Hezbollah and establishing a formal peace between the two nations.

items.title: European rates: Pricing disconnect persists – BNY
items.summary: BNY Strategist Geoff Yu argues that European rate markets still discount too many hikes for the European Central Bank (ECB), Bank of England (BoE) and Swiss National Bank (SNB) despite an improvement in global risk sentiment following the U.S.–Iran ceasefire.

items.title: USD: Dual Fed risks and ceasefire volatility – ING
items.summary: ING strategists Francesco Pesole, Frantisek Taborsky and Chris Turner note that the US Dollar (USD) has stabilised after Iran said the ceasefire was violated, but still see scope for renewed weakness.

items.title: US annual PCE inflation holds steady at 2.8% in February as expected
items.summary: Inflation in the United States, as measured by the change in the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index, held steady at 2.8% in February, the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) reported on Thursday. This print came in line with the market expectation.
